description: How Was I Hacked - A diagnostic tool to help crypto users understand how their wallet was compromised and learn security best practices

userStories.title: Project setup and welcome screen
userStories.description: As a hacked user visiting the site, I want to feel acknowledged and not judged so that I'm comfortable proceeding with the diagnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Web app scaffolded with mobile-first responsive design
Welcome screen displays empathetic messaging per PRD
Clear 'Get Started' call to action button
Navigation header with 'Learn Best Practices' link visible
Mental health app aesthetic - calm colors, generous whitespace
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database and session tracking
userStories.description: As a site operator, I want to collect anonymous usage data so that I can understand common attack patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Database schema created for Session, PathAttempt, PathStep, and Diagnosis tables per PRD data model
Session created on page load with user_hash (IP + User Agent hash)
No personally identifiable information stored
Migrations run successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wallet type selection screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select my wallet type so that the diagnostic asks relevant questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Four main wallet categories displayed: Browser Extension, Mobile, Hardware, Other
Sub-options for specific wallets (MetaMask, Rabby, Trust Wallet, Ledger, etc.) per PRD
Selection recorded to database (session.wallet_type, session.wallet_specific)
Smooth transition to next question
Mobile-friendly touch targets
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Value range collection screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to share how much I lost (optionally) so that the community can understand attack patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Value ranges displayed: Under $100, $100-$1,000, $1,000-$10,000, $10,000-$100,000, Over $100,000, Prefer not to say
Selection recorded to database (session.value_range)
Question feels non-intrusive per PRD copy
Smooth transition to next question
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Active vs noticed later branching question
userStories.description: As a user, I want to indicate when I noticed the hack so that the diagnostic can branch appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Question displayed: 'Were you actively using your wallet when the funds disappeared, or did you notice it later?'
Three options: 'I was using my wallet at the time', 'I noticed it later / wasn't using it', 'I'm not sure'
Selection creates new PathAttempt and PathStep records
'Actively using' leads to malicious transaction path
'Noticed later' and 'Not sure' lead to seed phrase compromise path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed phrase compromise path (Path A)
userStories.description: As a user who noticed the hack later, I want to answer questions about seed phrase storage so that I can identify how I was compromised.
acceptanceCriteria: A1: Seed phrase backup question with Yes/No/Not sure options
A2: Storage method question with options per PRD (physical secure, screenshot, notes, cloud, password manager, multiple)
A3: Password manager follow-up about unique passwords
A4: Copy/paste habits question
A4a: Where pasted follow-up (website, code file, between wallets)
A5: Downloaded files question
A5a: File type follow-up
A5b: File source follow-up (Discord/Telegram, email, website, job application)
A6: Wallet setup question (someone helped, bought wallet, self setup)
A7: Physical access fallback question
Each step recorded to PathStep table
Wallet-type-specific questions shown/hidden appropriately
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Malicious transaction path (Path B)
userStories.description: As a user who was actively using their wallet when hacked, I want to answer questions about how I found the site so that I can understand the attack.
acceptanceCriteria: B1: 'How did you find the site/app?' with options: social media, Google search, DM, email, typed URL directly
B2: 'What were you trying to do?' with options: airdrop, NFT mint, new dApp, token transaction, other
Each step recorded to PathStep table
Appropriate context stored for diagnosis messaging
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Probability engine
userStories.description: As the system, I want to calculate which diagnosis is most likely based on user answers so that I can guide them to the right conclusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial probability weights configured per PRD (Cloud Storage 20%, Phone Storage 15%, etc.)
Wallet type adjusts probabilities (e.g., hardware wallet reduces extension-related probabilities)
Each answer adjusts relevant diagnosis probabilities
When diagnosis rejected, that diagnosis weight set to 0.1x
System recalculates and returns to last fork on next-highest-probability path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diagnosis screens - seed phrase related
userStories.description: As a user who completed the diagnostic, I want to see a clear diagnosis with explanations and next steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloud Storage diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Phone Storage (screenshot) diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Digital Storage (notes/text) diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Password Reuse diagnosis screen with link to haveibeenpwned.com
Each diagnosis shows: authoritative statement, how attack works, empathy, action items
Links to Learn section and HackedWalletRecovery.com
'This doesn't seem right' option to try another path
Diagnosis recorded to database with accepted boolean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diagnosis screens - phishing and social engineering
userStories.description: As a user who was phished or socially engineered, I want to understand how the attack worked.
acceptanceCriteria: Phishing / Fake Site diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Exposed in Code (.env) diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Clipboard Compromise diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Social Engineering File (Discord/Telegram) diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Phishing Email diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Malicious Download diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Fake Job Scam diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Malicious Extension diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
All screens follow same format: statement, explanation, empathy, actions, links
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diagnosis screens - setup and transaction related
userStories.description: As a user who was compromised through setup or transactions, I want to understand what happened.
acceptanceCriteria: Compromised Setup diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Purchased Wallet Scam diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Compromised Hardware diagnosis screen with copy per PRD
Malicious Transaction diagnosis screen with context variations per PRD (social media, Google, DM, email, typed URL)
Final fallback screen for unidentified causes with encouragement to learn
Link to revoke.cash on transaction-related diagnoses
All screens follow same format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Path rejection and retry flow
userStories.description: As a user who doesn't think the diagnosis is right, I want to try another path so that I can find the real cause.
acceptanceCriteria: 'This doesn't seem right' button on all diagnosis screens
Rejection recorded (Diagnosis.accepted = false)
New PathAttempt created with incremented attempt_number
User taken to last fork point on next-highest-probability path
Previously rejected diagnoses have reduced probability
Harder follow-up questions asked on retry (e.g., 'Who has access to your seed phrase storage?')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Learn Best Practices section - core security
userStories.description: As a user, I want to learn security best practices so that I can protect myself going forward.
acceptanceCriteria: Section accessible from nav header at all times
Section accessible from all diagnosis screens
Seed Phrase Security topic with copy per PRD
Recognizing Scams topic with copy per PRD
Safe Browsing Habits topic with copy per PRD
Understanding Approvals topic with link to revoke.cash
Mobile-friendly layout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Learn Best Practices section - wallet architecture
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand hot/cold wallet architecture and multisig so that I can secure significant holdings.
acceptanceCriteria: Hot/Cold Wallet Model topic explaining checking vs savings account analogy per PRD
Multisig: The Gold Standard topic explaining 2-of-3 concept per PRD
Link to safe.global for multisig setup
What To Do After Being Hacked topic with steps per PRD
Security Checklist topic with actionable items per PRD
Link to HackedWalletRecovery.com as sister project
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Analytics tracking completion
userStories.description: As a site operator, I want to track user engagement so I can understand behavior patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Click to Learn section tracked (Diagnosis.clicked_learn)
Click to HackedWalletRecovery.com tracked (Diagnosis.clicked_hwr)
Full path recorded for each attempt in correct order
Multiple path attempts tracked with attempt_number weighting
Repeat visitors identified by user_hash
All data queryable for analytics per PRD queries list
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile optimization and polish
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to use the site comfortably on my phone so that I can get help immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: All screens tested on mobile viewport sizes
Touch-friendly buttons (minimum 44px tap targets)
Readable text without zooming
Smooth transitions between questions
Fast load times (under 3s on 3G)
No horizontal scrolling required
Calm, mental health app aesthetic maintained throughout
Typecheck passes
